name: public_health_procurement_shortcut_southport_auto_ben
premise: A regional manager wants an exception to policy; compliance says any carve-out must be written, time-limited, and auditable. In Public Health, the situation tagged internally around public health procurement shortcut southport auto ben has converged with local pressure in the Southport corridor. That label is not a full explanation, but it is how crews, counsel, and comms are indexing the case file right now. agents.name: Leila Park
agents.identity: I am Leila Park, the accountable executive on this bridge in Public Health. I own the signature line that turns a recommendation into an irreversible operational fact for the next phase.
agents.goal: I need a path that does not treat beneficence as disposable collateral every time autonomy spikes in urgency, but I also cannot pretend we have luxury time we do not have.

agents.name: Darius Kowalski
agents.identity: I am Darius Kowalski, the risk and equity liaison pushing back on silent harms in Public Health. I am tracking who gets protected last if we choose speed, silence, or a narrow definition of success.

choices: I will publish a constrained policy exception with named owners, expiry, and metrics—so beneficence does not get sacrificed by informal norms.
I will issue an emergency blanket authorization with transparent metrics afterward—so autonomy does not fail for lack of a single bold move.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The next irreversible system or legal step is imminent. Leila Park must choose under autonomy versus beneficence. What does Leila Park do?
